question: Compare the NuScale functional boundaries (Figure 1.2-2) with the RCS schematic (Figure 5.1-3) to identify where the NSSS boundary lies relative to RCS components.

nuscale_ch01_0032.title: Radioactive Waste Management System
nuscale_ch01_0032.summary: The section describes the radioactive waste management system at the NuScale Power Plant, noting that liquid, gaseous, and solid waste systems, as well as radiation monitoring, are detailed in Chapter 11. It also outlines the general arrangement of major structures, focusing on the Reactor Building (RXB), which is a reinforced, seismically designed structure housing the nuclear power modules and providing radiation protection for personnel.

nuscale_ch01_0069.title: Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams
nuscale_ch01_0069.summary: This section describes the use of simplified electrical, instrumentation and control (I&C), and mechanical drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report to visually clarify design information. It lists the relevant diagrams, figures, and legends used for both electrical/I&C and piping/instrumentation diagrams, and notes that site-specific diagrams and legends will be provided by COL applicants referencing the NuScale design certification.

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the NuScale design is located inside the containment vessel and is responsible for transporting heat from the reactor core to the steam generators using natural circulation. It includes components such as the reactor pressure vessel, pressurizer, and various valves, and features systems for removing noncondensible gases during both normal and emergency operations. The RCS also maintains the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary to prevent the release of radionuclides.

nuscale_ch01_0051.title: Plant Features of Special Interest
nuscale_ch01_0051.summary: This section describes key plant features of special interest in the NuScale Power Plant, including the Central Utility Building (CUB) and Diesel Generator Buildings (DGBs), which house essential but nonsafety-related systems and backup power equipment. It also highlights human factors considerations, such as fail-safe design, advanced automation, and digital controls, aimed at minimizing human error and optimizing operator workload, in compliance with regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0019.title: Operating Characteristics
nuscale_ch01_0019.summary: The NuScale Power Module (NPM) operates at full power using natural circulation for reactor coolant flow, eliminating the need for reactor coolant pumps. Its small, modular, integral pressurized water reactor design features passive safety systems, dedicated emergency cooling, and decay heat removal, all housed in a steel containment partially immersed in water for effective passive heat removal. The NPM allows for flexible power maneuvering using control rods and soluble boron, and its components are designed for modular fabrication and enhanced safety.

nuscale_ch01_0033.title: General Arrangement of Major Structures and Equipment
nuscale_ch01_0033.summary: This section describes the general arrangement of major structures and equipment at a NuScale Power Plant, focusing on the central placement and design of the Reactor Building (RXB). The RXB is a large, reinforced concrete structure designed for safety and radiation protection, housing the NuScale Power Modules (NPMs) in a common reactor pool with individual bays arranged in two rows. The section also references figures for detailed layouts and notes that some buildings, like the administration and training building, are outside the scope of the report.

nuscale_ch01_0018.title: Principal Design Criteria
nuscale_ch01_0018.summary: The section describes the principal design criteria for the NuScale plant, focusing on the Site Cooling Water System (SCWS) and the overall facility process. It outlines the reactor core’s heat transfer process, natural circulation of coolant, steam generation, and the flow of superheated steam to the turbine and condenser, emphasizing the system’s role in cooling and power generation.

nuscale_ch01_0118.title: 1.8 Interfaces with Certified Design
nuscale_ch01_0118.summary: This section explains the interface requirements between the NuScale Power Plant’s certified design and the site-specific design provided in the combined license (COL) application. It distinguishes between conceptual design information (CDI) for non-certified portions and COL information items, which are site-specific responsibilities. The section also notes that COL applicants must address all COL information items and provide a list of any departures from the certified design.

nuscale_ch01_0072.title: Combined License Information Items
nuscale_ch01_0072.summary: This section explains the interface requirements between the NuScale Power Plant certified design and the site-specific design provided in the Combined License (COL) application. It distinguishes between conceptual design information (CDI) for non-certified plant portions and COL information items, which are site-specific responsibilities of the COL applicant. The section also notes that COL applicants must address all COL information items and provide a list of any departures from the certified design.

nuscale_ch05_0007.title: Integrity of Reactor Coolant Boundary
nuscale_ch05_0007.summary: This section describes the design features of the NuScale Power Module (NPM) that ensure the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) throughout the plant’s life, in accordance with regulatory definitions. It details the components and systems included in the RCPB, such as the reactor pressure vessel, containment isolation valves, safety valves, and associated piping, emphasizing their roles in maintaining pressure containment and system safety.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as specific ASME code cases relevant to reactor component materials and safety requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0011.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Inservice Inspection and Testing
nuscale_ch05_0011.summary: This section describes the materials and standards used for threaded fasteners in key reactor coolant system components, emphasizing the use of Alloy 718 for its compatibility and stress corrosion cracking resistance. It also outlines the requirements for preservice and inservice inspection and testing of reactor coolant pressure boundary components, specifying adherence to the latest ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) editions as mandated by regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0004.title: Containment Type
nuscale_ch01_0004.summary: The NuScale containment vessel is a cylindrical, steel, ASME-certified pressure vessel designed to withstand high-pressure events and operates under vacuum during normal conditions, eliminating the need for insulation. The NuScale reactor is a passive, integral small modular pressurized water reactor (SMR) with natural circulation and no external piping for steam generators or pressurizer, and each module produces about 50 MWe, with a 12-module plant yielding a net output of approximately 570 MWe.

nuscale_ch01_0160.title: DSRS 3.11, Rev. 0: Environmental Qualification of Mechanical and Electrical EquipmentII.25 Design and Procurement Specifications
nuscale_ch01_0160.summary: This section outlines NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory criteria related to the environmental qualification of mechanical and electrical equipment, as well as design and procurement specifications. It notes that certain requirements, such as DSRS 3.11 II.25, are not applicable to NuScale and are instead items for the COL applicant, while other standards (e.g., SRP 3.12 and 3.13) are confirmed as being met. Additionally, guidance specific to BWR plants is marked as not applicable to NuScale’s design.

nuscale_ch01_0068.title: Electrical and Instrumentation and Control Drawings
nuscale_ch01_0068.summary: This section explains that the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report includes simplified electrical, instrumentation, and control (I&C) drawings to visually clarify design information, with component positions not necessarily indicating operational states unless specified. It references tables and figures listing and explaining the symbols used in these diagrams, and notes that applicants referencing the NuScale design must provide additional site-specific diagrams and legends as needed.

nuscale_ch01_0861.title: SRP 19.5, Rev 0: Adequacy of Design features and functional capabilities identified and described for withstanding Aircraft ImpactsAll Various
nuscale_ch01_0861.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory criteria, specifically noting that the design features and functional capabilities for withstanding aircraft impacts fully conform to SRP 19.5, Rev 0 requirements. It also details partial or full conformance with other related SRP sections and Interim Staff Guidance, with specific exceptions noted where criteria are not applicable to NuScale’s design.
